methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $96M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Antares is a Series B nuclear energy company founded by Jordan Bramble and Julia DeWahl that has raised $96M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 3 public firms (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private nuclear energy companies including Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Fuse Energy, and Aalo Atomics.

A stage-progression methodology proves applicable, using the $96M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the subsequent funding round. This method yields an implied valuation of $182M. A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient comparable datapoints at identical or adjacent stages within nuclear energy—while four same-sector peers exist, fewer than three meet the narrow filters of stage and valuation tier, making sector-wide median calculation unreliable.

The triangulated analysis, supported by one quantifiable method, indicates a valuation range of $155M (low) to $210M (high), with a median point of $182M. Key uncertainties include the reliability of raised capital as a valuation anchor absent current market terms, and the narrow data coverage within early-stage nuclear energy specifically. Additional signals—recent comparable financings, burn rate, and technical milestone achievement—would strengthen confidence in this range.
